title: 10 best herbal creams for amenorrhea

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Dong Quai's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to hormonal imbalances and amenorrhea by promoting a healthy environment for hormone production.

Vasodilatory properties: Dong Quai's ability to dilate blood vessels helps improve circulation, which can increase blood flow to the uterus and ovaries, supporting hormonal balance and regular menstrual cycles.

Estrogen-regulating properties: Dong Quai contains isoflavones that can help regulate estrogen levels in the body, which can be beneficial for women with amenorrhea caused by hormonal imbalances or deficiencies.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica sinensis, also known as dong quai, creams helps with amenorrhea because of its ability to balance and regulate hormones in the body. The cream's active compounds, including ferulic acid and ligustilide, stimulate the pituitary gland to release follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH) and luteinizing hormone (LH), which are essential for menstrual cycle regulation. Additionally, dong quai's anti-inflammatory properties may help to soothe and calm the uterine lining, promoting a healthy menstrual cycle and reducing the risk of amenorrhea. This natural remedy supports menstrual health and fertility.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: These compounds, particularly ferulic acid and caffeic acid, help regulate menstrual cycles by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the uterus, promoting regular menstrual flow.

Iridoid glycosides (specifically, loganin and secologanin): These compounds may help stimulate uterine contractions and regulate hormone balance, leading to improved menstrual regularity and reduced symptoms of amenorrhea.

Coumarins (specifically, scopoletin and scopoletin glucoside): These compounds may help regulate hormone balance, reduce inflammation, and promote uterine contractions, all of which can contribute to the normalization of menstrual cycles and relief from amenorrhea symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high content of ferulic acid, which has been shown to stimulate uterine contractions and promote menstrual flow.
Leaves: They are used due to their rich content of flavonoids and alkaloids, which have been found to have a regulatory effect on the menstrual cycle and help alleviate menstrual irregularities.
Rhyzomes: They are used due to their high content of ferulic acid and other bioactive compounds, which have been shown to have a regulatory effect on the menstrual cycle and promote menstrual flow.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh angelica sinensis roots and clean them thoroughly in cold running water for 5 minutes.
Dry the roots in a low-temperature oven at 40°c for 2 hours to preserve their properties.
Grind 10 grams of dried angelica sinensis roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with 100 grams of coconut oil and 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a smooth consistency.

remedy_properties: Vasoprotective effects: Chaste tree cream's vasoprotective effects help to strengthen and stabilize blood vessels, which can improve uterine blood flow and hormonal regulation, thereby addressing underlying causes of amenorrhea.

Hormone-balancing properties: The cream contains compounds that have a regulating effect on the endocrine system, particularly on estrogen and progesterone levels, helping to restore normal menstrual cycles in cases of amenorrhea caused by hormonal imbalances.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Vitex agnus castus, also known as chaste tree, creams helps with amenorrhea because of its ability to regulate hormonal imbalances. It contains a compound called aucubin, which helps to stimulate the production of luteinizing hormone (LH), a key hormone involved in ovulation and menstrual cycles. By promoting a healthy balance of hormones, Vitex agnus castus creams can help to restore regular menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea. This makes it a popular natural remedy for women experiencing menstrual irregularities.
remedy_constituents: Agnuside: Agnuside is a diterpenoid compound found in Vitex agnus castus, which helps regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate amenorrhea by stimulating the release of gonadotropin-releasing hormone (GnRH) and luteinizing hormone (LH), thereby promoting ovulation and menstrual flow.

Agnuscastin: Agnuscastin is a flavonoid glycoside present in Vitex agnus castus, which helps regulate hormonal balance and alleviate amenorrhea by modulating the activity of GnRH and LH, ultimately leading to the normalization of menstrual cycles.

Casticin: Casticin is a flavonoid compound found in Vitex agnus castus, which helps alleviate amenorrhea by stimulating the release of GnRH and LH, promoting ovulation, and regulating menstrual cycles due to its estrogenic and progesterogenic activities.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high content of iridoid glycosides, which are believed to have a regulating effect on hormonal imbalances.
Leaves: They are utilized for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help soothe and balance the body's systems.
Seeds: They are employed for their hormonal balancing effects, particularly in regulating estrogen levels and menstrual cycles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried vitex agnus castus flowers from a trusted source to ensure quality and potency.
Combine the dried flowers with 20ml of carrier oil in a glass jar to create the infusion.
Place the jar in a warm dark place and let it steep for 2 weeks to allow the herbs to infuse into the oil.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth into another glass container discarding the solids to produce the herbal infusion.
Mix 10g of beeswax with 10ml of coconut oil and the herbal infusion in a double boiler to create a smooth cream.

remedy_parts: Roots: They are used to treat amenorrhea due to their ability to regulate hormones and improve menstrual flow.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root, dried to 10 moisture content for best results.
Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a spice grinder at 10 seconds per gram.
Combine 500 grams of distilled water with 100 grams of the ground root powder in a stainless steel vessel.
Heat the mixture to 90 degrees celsius, then reduce heat to 60 degrees celsius and simmer for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and add 20 grams of beeswax to thicken.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried root of althaea officinalis in the spring season when it is in full bloom.
Combine the harvested root with 500ml of distilled water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 30 minutes to extract the root's medicinal properties.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl and discard the solids.
Mix 100 grams of emulsifying wax with 100 grams of coconut oil and 50 grams of beeswax to create a cream base.

remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers and 100 grams of vegetable oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil.

Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container and discard the solids.

Blend the infused oil mixture with the melted wax mixture and pour into small containers for storage and application.

Combine the ginkgo biloba powder with 100 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves, wash them gently and chop into small pieces.
Combine 20 grams of chopped melissa leaves with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture at room temperature for 14 days to allow for gentle extraction of bioactive compounds.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids, then mix in 10 grams of beeswax pellets with 20 milliliters of coconut oil.
Pour the mixture into a heat-proof container and melt at low temperature (30-40°c) for 10-15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milkweed creams helps with amenorrhea because they stimulate hormonal balance in the body. The phytoestrogens present in milkweed, particularly isoflavones, mimic the effects of estrogen in the body, helping to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea. Additionally, milkweed's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce uterine inflammation and promote a healthy endometrial lining, further supporting menstrual regularity. By promoting hormonal balance and reducing inflammation, herbal milkweed creams can help restore normal menstruation patterns in women with amenorrhea.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Milkweed cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as cardenolides, help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with menstrual cramps, which can contribute to amenorrhea.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant properties of milkweed cream, including flavonoids and saponins, can help protect the body from oxidative stress and promote healthy hormone balance, which may aid in regulating menstruation and alleviating symptoms of amenorrhea.

Hormone-regulating properties: Milkweed contains compounds like cardenolides that have been shown to modulate hormonal activity, including estrogen levels, which is essential for regulating the menstrual cycle and treating conditions like amenorrhea.
remedy_parts: Roots: Milkweed roots contain saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making them effective in soothing menstrual cramps and reducing inflammation associated with amenorrhea.

Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of milkweed plant are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to regulate hormones and improve uterine blood flow, making them useful for treating amenorrhea caused by hormonal imbalances.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried asclepias syriaca root powder from a reputable supplier.
Combine the harvested powder with 100 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture for 30 minutes over low heat, stirring occasionally, to facilitate infusion.
Remove the double boiler from the heat and let it steep for an additional 1 hour.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar, discarding solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams help with amenorrhea because they contain soothing and anti-inflammatory properties that can ease hormonal imbalances and alleviate stress, two common underlying causes of this condition. The calming effects of chamomile can also help regulate the menstrual cycle, promoting healthy ovulation and reducing symptoms such as cramps and mood swings. Additionally, chamomile's antioxidant properties can help protect the uterus and promote overall reproductive health, making it a natural and effective solution for women experiencing amenorrhea.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce pelvic congestion and cramping associated with menstrual irregularities, which can contribute to amenorrhea.
Sedative property: The sedative nature of chamomile cream can calm the uterine muscle, regulating hormonal balance and promoting a normal menstrual cycle, thereby treating amenorrhea.

Antioxidant property: Chamomile's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress, which may contribute to hormone imbalance and menstrual irregularities, including amenorrhea, by protecting the body from free radicals and promoting overall reproductive health.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in apigenin and luteolin, the flowers help reduce inflammation and relax uterine muscles, alleviating menstrual cramps and promoting regular menstruation.
Roots: Containing high amounts of flavonoids and coumarins, the roots have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help soothe pelvic pain and regulate hormonal imbalances.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and combine them with 100ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 3 hours, stirring occasionally, until the oil has infused with the chamomilla's volatile compounds.
Strain the oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Combine 20g of beeswax pellets with 10ml of the infused oil in a small saucepan and heat gently over low heat until melted and smooth.
Pour the melted wax-oil mixture into sterilized glass jars, allowing it to cool and solidify before sealing tightly for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with amenorrhea because they promote a sense of calmness and relaxation in the body. Amenorrhea is often linked to hormonal imbalances, stress, and anxiety, which can disrupt menstrual cycles. The soothing properties of lavender help to regulate hormones, reduce anxiety and stress levels, and improve sleep quality. By creating a tranquil environment for the body, English lavender creams can encourage the return of menstruation by normalizing hormone production and promoting a healthy reproductive cycle. This natural approach can be particularly beneficial for women experiencing amenorrhea due to emotional or psychological factors.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-inflammatory Properties: Lavender's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to Amenorrhea by addressing underlying hormonal imbalances.

Hormone Regulation: The estrogens in lavender have been shown to regulate hormones and balance menstrual cycles, helping to alleviate symptoms of Amenorrhea such as irregular periods or absent periods.

Stress Relief and Calming Effects: Lavender's calming effects can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for Amenorrhea. By promoting relaxation and reducing cortisol levels, lavender creams may help restore normal menstrual function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lavender roots have been traditionally used to regulate menstrual cycles and ease symptoms associated with amenorrhea due to their rich content of phytoestrogens, which help balance hormone levels.
Barks: The bark of the English lavender plant contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that can help soothe and calm the uterine lining, promoting a regular menstrual cycle.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers, weighing approximately 200 grams for this recipe.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm, well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator for 1-2 hours.
Combine dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 500 milliliters of sweet almond oil and let it infuse for 2 weeks shaking occasionally.
Strain the infused oil mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding solids after 30 minutes.
Mix the strained oil with 20 grams of beeswax, melting it in a double boiler then stirring until combined and cooled completely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Beach rose creams helps with amenorrhea because they contain a unique blend of herbs that work in harmony to stimulate hormonal balance. The creams' primary ingredient, beach rose extract, has been traditionally used to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea such as irregular periods or complete cessation of menstruation. Additionally, the creams may also help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for amenorrhea. By addressing these underlying issues, beach rose creams can help restore a healthy menstrual cycle and promote overall reproductive well-being.
remedy_properties: Emmenagogic properties: Beach rose creams have emmenagogic properties, which means they can stimulate menstrual flow and relieve delayed or absent menstruation (amenorrhea) by increasing uterine contractions and improving blood circulation to the reproductive organs.

Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic compounds in beach rose creams help relax the uterus and alleviate cramping, spasms, and discomfort associated with amenorrhea, making it easier for the body to initiate menstruation.

Estrogen-regulating properties: Beach rose creams may also help regulate estrogen levels, which can become imbalanced in cases of amenorrhea. By normalizing estrogen production, beach rose creams may help stimulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms such as mood swings, breast tenderness, and hot flashes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which help to regulate hormonal balance and ease menstrual cramps.
Barks: Contains phenolic compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, reducing pelvic pain and discomfort associated with amenorrhea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of fresh rosa rugosa petals, ensuring they are free from pesticides and heavy metals.
Dry the petals in a warm oven at 50°c for 24 hours or air-dry them for 7 days.
Combine 100g of dried rosa rugosa petals with 300ml of organic coconut oil and infuse for 14 days.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth, retaining the oil and discarding the solids, taking approximately 30 minutes.
Mix the strained oil with 50g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter, heating at 60°c until smooth and creamy.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for amenorrhea are topical preparations that combine the therapeutic properties of herbs with emollients to stimulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea. They often feature herbs like clary sage, rosemary, and ginseng, which have been traditionally used to regulate menstrual cycles. Examples include clary sage cream and ginseng cream. These creams can help improve lives by stimulating menstrual cycles, reducing stress, and promoting overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to amenorrhea that are treated with herbal creams are endometriosis, polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS), and dysmenorrhea. Herbal creams containing ingredients like chasteberry, Dong quai, and wild yam help alleviate symptoms such as irregular periods, heavy bleeding, and pelvic pain by promoting hormonal balance and improving uterine health. These creams can be used in conjunction with conventional treatments to provide natural relief from menstrual disorders.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with amenorrhea is a blend of Clary Sage, Peppermint, and Wild Yam. Clary Sage helps to regulate hormones and reduce stress, while Peppermint aids in digestion and circulation, which can improve menstrual flow. Wild Yam contains diosgenin, a plant-based estrogen that can stimulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea. These creams can be applied topically to the lower abdomen or taken internally as a supplement, but consult a healthcare professional before use.
